userFeedback: The block editor body was a plain form (TextControl, TextareaControl, etc.) with no visual resemblance to the card-testimonial.php template. The user expected the editor to mirror the card design with inline-editable fields styled to match the frontend output.
rootCause: The Developer Agent treated the block body as a generic data-entry form instead of reading the design reference (parts/card-testimonial.php) and replicating its visual structure. The Planning Agent specified 'use template part as design reference for editor layout' but did not produce explicit markup mapping (e.g., 'name field → h3 with font-heading class, message → blockquote with italic styling'). The Developer Agent took the path of least resistance: WordPress form components with labels.
fixSummary: Rewrote TestimonialCPT.tsx to render an article element matching the card template's structure: bg-accent rounded-3xl card, RichText h3 for name with font-heading, program taxonomy term display, RichText blockquote for message with italic styling, and a 'Watch the Video' indicator when video is configured. Video configuration controls moved to InspectorControls sidebar. The editor now visually previews what the frontend card looks like.

affectedInstructions.currentGuidance: Says all meta fields must appear in both block body AND sidebar. Does not specify that block body controls should visually match the frontend template.
affectedInstructions.suggestedChange: Add a subsection: '### Block Body as Visual Preview'. When a design reference (template part or Figma) is provided, the block body MUST visually replicate the frontend layout using the same CSS classes and HTML structure. Use RichText components for inline-editable text fields styled to match the design, not generic TextControl/TextareaControl form fields. Configuration-only fields (toggles, media selectors, source pickers) belong in InspectorControls or the sidebar panel, NOT in the visual preview area. The block body is a WYSIWYG preview with editable slots, not a data entry form.

affectedInstructions.currentGuidance: Planning agent maps meta fields to UI components + placement (body vs sidebar) but does not require explicit markup/class mapping from the design reference.
affectedInstructions.suggestedChange: When a design reference template part is provided for a CPT block, the planning spec MUST include a 'editorMarkupMap' section that explicitly maps each meta field to its visual representation in the editor: the HTML tag, CSS classes, component type (RichText vs TextControl), and the exact position within the card layout. Example: { field: 'name', tag: 'h3', classes: 'font-heading text-[1.5rem] md:text-[2.5rem] leading-[1.1] text-charcoal', editorComponent: 'RichText', placement: 'body-inline' }. Non-visual fields (videoSource, videoFile, videoId) should be mapped to InspectorControls with placement: 'sidebar-only' or 'inspector'.

affectedInstructions.currentGuidance: Developer agent instructions say to create body-level controls for primary editing. Does not enforce visual fidelity to the design reference.
affectedInstructions.suggestedChange: Add rule: 'When the planning spec includes a design reference or editorMarkupMap, the block body component MUST render HTML/CSS that visually matches the reference. Use RichText with allowedFormats for text fields, replicate the card's wrapper classes (bg-accent, rounded-3xl, padding, flexbox layout), and show read-only previews for taxonomy terms and computed state (e.g., video indicator). NEVER render a plain form with labels when a visual design reference exists.'

affectedInstructions.currentGuidance: Says 'Use template part as design reference for editor layout if designReference.templatePart is provided. Read the template part to understand the visual structure, then design the editor controls to match that layout logically.'
affectedInstructions.suggestedChange: Strengthen to: 'Read the template part and produce an explicit editorMarkupMap in the planning output. The editor block body MUST render the same visual structure as the template part — same HTML tags, same Tailwind classes, same layout hierarchy. The editor is a live preview of the card, not a form. Text fields become RichText components; taxonomy terms become read-only labels fetched via useSelect; configuration fields (toggles, media pickers) go to InspectorControls. If the template shows a rounded card with accent background, the editor must show a rounded card with accent background.'

lessonsLearned: CPT block bodies should be WYSIWYG previews of the frontend card design, not data-entry forms
When a design reference (template part) exists, the planning spec must produce an explicit markup map that the developer can follow 1:1

Form-style controls (TextControl, TextareaControl, ToggleGroupControl) belong in InspectorControls or the PluginDocumentSettingPanel sidebar, not in the block body when a visual design reference is provided
RichText with allowedFormats=[] is the correct pattern for single-line editable text in a visual preview (replaces TextControl)
RichText without allowedFormats restriction is correct for rich content fields like testimonial messages
Taxonomy terms should be fetched via useSelect/coreStore and displayed as read-only text in the visual preview
